license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.bbexijfn
title: High-Throughput Beta-glucuronidase (GUS) assay for Phaeodactylum tricornutum
author: Erin Garza

steps_raw.instruction: Transfer 250 μl of each P. tricornutum culture to a 96-well plate and centrifuge at 3000 x g for 10 min. Discard supernatant.
To lyse the cells, add 150 µl bacterial protein extraction reagent (B-PER, ThermoFisher) to each well and mix by pipetting.
Centrifuge plate for 10 min at 3000 x g. Transfer supernatants to a new 96-well plate, being careful not to disturb the cell debris.

Transfer 50 µl of each lysate to a new plate and add 125 µl GUS extraction buffer + 1 mM MUG to each well. Incubate the plate for 1 h at 37°C. GUS extraction buffer= 50 mM NaPO 4 H 2 (pH 7), 0.1% Triton X-100, and 10 µM βME
To stop the reaction, add 150 µl GUS stop buffer (0.2 M Na 2 CO 3 ) to each well and mix by pipetting.
Transfer 200 µl quenched reaction to an opaque 96-well plate.
Determine fluorescence using a plate reader. Settings: excitation- 360 nm; emission- 440 nm.
If fluorescence readings are too high to get a readout, dilute with additional stop buffer.
Use remaining cell lysates to perform a BCA assay (or an equivalent assay) to normalize the GUS activity to total cell protein for each culture.
